answer.text:
many to one: 導致cpu只看的到 kernel 也就是1個，而導致沒有用到multi-core的優勢
one to one :可以利用到multi-core的優勢
one to one 可以有更好用到multi-core的優勢，因為這樣可以有更好的scalability，可以隨著number of cores去擴展

1 to 1: 1 kernel thread serves 1 user thread.
N to 1: 1 kernel thread can serve more than 1 user thread.
N to 1 allows better utilization of multi-core processor since it could use the core frequently by switch to another thread.

one-to-one, because  many-to-one is dependent on single kernel, so it's vulnerable; in contrast, one-to-one can have many kernal one user coincided one kernel, means they run independently or currently on multi-core processors.
one to one, since user level thread can only use single processor, only use more kernel-thread can use multi-core processors.
One-to-one可以使用multithreading model，因為OS可以直觀的看到所有的thread，如果有thread被block的時候就可以執行其他thread；然而many-to-one model會因為一個thread被block而導致，整個process都被block，因為OS無法分別哪些user thread是被block的，所以會全部block住

many-to-one means many user-level threads is assigned to one kernel-level thread
one-t-one means one userlevel thread is assigned to one kernel-level thread

one-to-one, because each user-level thread can run on cpu when its corresponding kernel-level thread runs on cpu, it doesn't have to compete with other user-level threads.

Many-to-One 為多個 user threads 對應到一個 kernel thread
One-to-One 為一個 user thread 對應到一個 kernel thread
One-to-One 可以善用 multi-core processors，因為他可以讓多個 kernel thread 平行運作，一次服務多個 user thread。
Many-to-One 只有一個 kernel thread 的情況下則無法，因為只有 kernel thread 可以平行運作。

One-to-One.
因為 user-level threads 會被 OS 視為是單一個 process，即使有多條 user-level threads，只對應到一條 kernel-level thread 也無法使用到 multi-core；一條 user-level thread 直接對應一條 kernel-level thread 才可以發揮 multi-core 的優勢。

1.一個是多個user-level thread對一個kernal-level thread，一個是一個user-level thread對一個kernal-level thread
2.one to one 比較好，可以運行在多核心上，many to one 因為只對一個kernal-level thread，所以那些user-level thread只能在一個核心運行
one-to-one will have better utilization of multi-core processor because each thread can allocate into different core and run concurrently. while many-to-one can only run in one core.

One to one
the many-to-one is still be seemed as one , didn't make good use of the multi-core processors.

One-to-One, because we can let a process (or a thread) run on different core then increase the scability when run in the multi-core processors, which is efficient. For Many-to-One, many threads bounded to one process, it can't better utilize the multi-core processors.

One to one, because in Many-to-one when a user thread need to wait for an I/O other user thread in the same group would also be blocked. And one-to-one would only block its own kernel thread, so one-to-one would have better utilization

Many-to-One - many user threads can use the same kernel thread to be executed.
One-to-One - one kernel thread is created for each user thread, so only one user thread can use the same kernel thread to be executed. This model allows better utilization of multi-core processors, because kernel manages kernel threads and it can execute them on different cpu cores. However the kernel is not aware of user-level threads, so it can not execute them on different cores, thus many-to-one model can not run truly parallel on a multi-core processors.
one to one
1:1 can use multi-core processors, since M:1 only has one kernel-level thread, and os can only schedule kernel-level thread, so this M user-level thread can't use multi-core cpu.

Threads are an execution flow. An user thread cannot use multi-core processors, but entrusting it to a kernel thread allows the kernel thread to use multi-core processors to execute it. 
If we attribute many user threads to a kernel one, he will be able to use the multiple cores to execute them all. If we attribute one thread to a kernel one, he will also be able to use the multiple cores to execute it, but that makes the throughput much more efficient (if we have N user threads, it would be more efficient to use 1 kernel to execute them rather than N ones)

Many to one means(N:1) Many user-level threads for 1 kernel level threads while One-to-one(1:1) means 1 user-level thread for 1 kernel-level threads. 

The first one allows better utilization of multi-core processors because it separates the process in multiple threads that can works asynchronously thanks to the multi-core processors.

One-to-One 會有更好的利用率。多核處理器對應的是 kernel level threads 而非 user level threads。假設 kernel threads 有多條，且單一核對應一條 kernel thread。如果多條 user threads 同時對應到單一條 kernel thread 則等於多條 user threads 只能使用單一核。反之，如果是 1:1 的對應方式則每一條 user thread 都可以透過各自對應的 kernel thread 獨自只用單一核。這使得 1:1 mapping 方式可以有更好的多核處理器利用率。
many to one have better utilization of multi-core processors, since one thread can only work on a processor, so in one-to-one case,  increase the number of processor do not have any benefit.

many-to-one是N個user-level和一個kernel-level，可以多個user和kernel互動。
one-to-one是一個user-level和一個kernel-level，採一對一，且有更好的使用率在多核心processors，因為many-to-one只有一個kernel，所以所有user共享，而one-to-one不需要共享。
Many-to-one的multithreading model更能利用多核的優勢，因為many-to-one可以分工並行處理，share memory也不用再額外撰寫inter-process的一些手段，creat與switch也較容易，造成較少的CPU overhead。

Although the best answer will be many too many...

Here I will say the one-to-one, since in many-to-one models, we use many user level threads, but only 1 kernel level thread, this will not utilize multi-core processors, since we run many threads on only 1 kernel= 1 core. So relatively speaking, the 1-1 multithreading model allow better utilization.

many to one 
因為她在multi的利用率較高，降低等待的時間以及行為

One-to-One multithreading models allows better utilization of multi-core processors, because CPU can only see the kernel level thread, One-to-One multithreading models means the number of kernel level thread are equal to user level thread, CPU can run more cores concurrently, while  Many-to-one multithreading models means there are many user level threads but one kernel level thread, CPU can only run one core while there are more than one core.

Many-to-one is model with many user-level threads and one kernel-level thread. 
One-to-one is model with one user-level thread and one kernel-level thread.
One-to-one allows better utilization of multi-core processors because the proportion of the kernel-level thread is higher in this model.

1-1 is better because many user-level threads is only seen as 1 process.

One-to-One is better because 如果many-to-one這樣只有一個kernel thread,沒辦法使用multi-core
 processors

'one-to-one' multithreading model has better utilization of the multi-core processors as it allows the resources to be allocated directly without having to wait but it may have longer process time but will be executed thoroughly.
One-to-One allows better utilization of multi-core processors, because when using Many-to-One, only one actual cpu is associated to it, it can not run threads simutaneously.
Many-to-one, since if one thread is finish it can switch to another.
One-to-One讓multi-core processors可以有更好的利用，因為many-to-one產生出的多個thread都在user mode裡面，但是os是看不到這些threads的，所以無法利用多核心的優勢。

Many user threads is controlled by one kernel thread is called "Many-to-one".
One user thread is controlled by one kernel thread is called "One-to-One".

One-to-One allows better utilization of multi-core processors, since an application with multi-threads is controlled by multiple kernel threads, and we can allocate these threads cpu resources concurrently using multi-core processor system.
one to one, because os only see one process whatever having how many user-level threads
One-to-One, because processors will consider Many-to-One as a whole, so processors will not distribute the resource for the thread inside. it's not able to utilize the multi-core processor.

Many-to-one is better, 因為可以同時處理多個thread.
1:1更好，每一thread可以對應1核心，M:1則是多對應1

1:1 model:kernel看得到process內部的所有threads，scheduler可以分配每個threads到不同cores，可以parellel。
所以1:1 model比較適合muti-core processors。

One-to-one. Because only kernel-level thread can take advantage of multi-core. Therefore One-to-one allows better utilization of multi-core processors.

Many-to-one: If one user thread is broken, than it has another user thread to execute. OS think kernel thread it's normal.
one-to-one: If the only user thread is broken, that it can't not execute. OS thinks kernel thread it's broken.

The one-to-one multithreading model can have better utilization. For the many-to-one multithreading model, user threads are managed by the application instead of the OS, so the OS does not know the existence of these threads. In contrast, the OS knows the existence of the thread in the one-to-one multithreading model, so it can manage the thread and use multiple core at the same time.

question_context.prompt: Compare the 'Many-to-One' and 'One-to-One' multithreading models. Which one allows better utilization of multi-core processors, and why?
question_context.reference_answer:
a. Many-to-One maps many user threads to one kernel thread.
b. One-to-One maps each user thread to a kernel thread.
c. One-to-One is better for multi-core because it allows multiple threads to run in parallel; Many-to-One can only run one thread at a time because the kernel only sees one entity.
